_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about the disagreements we sometimes have with our friends and classmates, and how the way we act and think can change what happens next.

_source.w: We are learning about the disagreements we sometimes have with our friends and classmates, and how the way we act and think can change what happens next.
y: Conflicts happen to everyone — understanding them helps us handle disagreements without hurting ourselves or others.
When we understand how our behaviour affects outcomes, we gain the power to make things better instead of worse.
Learning to manage conflict now gives us skills we will use in friendships, family life and beyond.
eg: We brainstorm types of conflicts that happen between friends — like arguing over a game or feeling hurt by something someone said.
We name the emotions that come up during a disagreement, like frustration, sadness or anger.
We look at two different responses to the same conflict and talk about which one leads to a better outcome.
We role-play a playground argument and practise using calm words instead of shouting.
We draw a comic strip showing a conflict and two possible endings — one where someone stays angry and one where they talk it out.
We share stories about times we resolved a disagreement and what we learned from the experience.
